zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Thomas de Payne III, Knights Templar於1200年出生在英國萊希斯特斯爾馬可特 博斯沃斯時，他的父親Sir Thomas Payne II, Knights Templar30歲，母親Lady Elizabeth Marguerite25歲。他於大約1240年與Elizabeth de Payne在英國萊希斯特斯爾馬可特 博斯沃斯結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1247年在英國萊希斯特斯爾享年47歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Thomas de Payne III, Knights Templar于1200年出生在英国萊希斯特斯爾馬可特 博斯沃斯时，他的父亲Sir Thomas Payne II, Knights Templar30岁，母亲Lady Elizabeth Marguerite25岁。他于大约1240年与Elizabeth de Payne在英国萊希斯特斯爾馬可特 博斯沃斯结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1247年在英国萊希斯特斯爾享年47岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Payne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English and Old French personal name Pai(e)n, Pagen (from Latin Paganus), a fairly common personal name among Normans. It derived from a word that originally meant ‘villager, rustic’, later ‘heathen’, but it had doubtless lost these connotations in its use as a late medieval personal name. This name has also been established in Ireland since the 14th century.

History: Thomas Payne, who was a freeman of Plymouth Colony in 1639, was the founder of a large American family, which included Robert Treat Paine (1731–1814), one of the signers of the Declaration of Independence. See also   Paine  .

variants.name: Payn
meanings: English (Kent): variant of   Payne  .

variants.name: Payen
meanings: French: from the Old French personal name Pagen, Paien, from Latin Paganus, from paganus ‘country-dweller’, a derivative of pagus ‘of or relating to the countryside’, possibly given to a child not yet baptized (because of its later sense of ‘pagan, heathen’). Alternatively, a nickname for a country-born person. This surname is also found in the West Indies (mainly Haiti) and Mauritius.
English: variant of   Payne  .

variants.name: Pagan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Juan, Angel, Carlos, Rafael, Ramon, Pedro, Francisco, Jesus, Miguel, Julio.

Spanish and Catalan: Castilianized form (Pagán) of Catalan Pagà, from the Late Latin personal name Paganus, which as a derivative of Latin pagus ‘outlying village’ originally meant ‘villager’ (see Payne ). Alternatively, in some cases, a topographic name from Catalan pagà ‘heath’ (from the same Latin word pagus).
English: from the Middle English and Old French personal name Pai(e)n, Pagen, from Late Latin Paganus (see Payne , compare 1 above).

variants.name: Paynes
meanings: English: variant of Payne , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Paine
meanings: English: variant of   Payne  .

History: The author of the republican treatise The Rights of Man, Thomas Paine (1737–1809), left England for North America in the mid 1770s, where he became involved in the movement that led to independence. His pamphlet of 1776, Common Sense, influenced the Declaration of Independence and furnished some of the arguments justifying it.

variants.name: Pank
meanings: English (Norfolk): perhaps from an unrecorded Middle English personal name Pank(e). It could be a shortened form of Pancok, which is probably a pet form of Pane (see Payne ).
Sorbian: nickname from a diminutive of pan ‘mister, lord, landlord’ (see Pan ). Compare Panke .

variants.name: Pane
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Antonio, Salvatore, Aldo, Carmela, Carmine, Ciro, Concetta, Enzo, Giuseppe, Mario, Armando, Claudio, Macario, Remigio.

Italian: from pane ‘bread’, used as a metonymic occupational name for a baker or as a nickname meaning ‘good thing’, and also as a personal name. This surname is also found in southern France and Corsica.
English: variant of   Payne  .

variants.name: Panes
meanings: English (Somerset): variant of Payne , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Pannell
meanings: English (southeastern):
possibly from Middle English panel, panel(l)e ‘cloth, saddle-cloth, saddle’ or ‘pane (of glass)’, hence a metonymic occupational name for a maker of such items.
from the Middle English personal name Paynel (Old French Payenel, Latin Paganellus), a pet form of Payne (from Latin Paganus).

variants.name: Penson
meanings: English:
patronymic from the Middle English female personal name Penn, a pet form of Pernel, or perhaps from the Middle English personal name Payne + -son. See Penn , Parnell .

habitational name from Penson in North Huish (Devon), which derives from the Anglo-Norman French personal name Payn(e) (see Payne ) + Middle English toun ‘village, town, settlement’ (Old English tūn ‘farmstead, estate’).

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Thomas de Payne III, Knights Templar was born in 1200, in Market Bosworth, Leicestershire, England, his father, Sir Thomas Payne II, Knights Templar, was 30 and his mother, Lady Elizabeth Marguerite, was 25. He married Elizabeth de Payne about 1240, in Market Bosworth, Leicestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1247, in Leicestershire, England, at the age of 47.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas de Payne III, Knights Templarが1200年にイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアマーケト ボスウオルスで生まれた時，父Sir Thomas Payne II, Knights Templarは30歳，母Lady Elizabeth Margueriteは25歳であった。約1240年にイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアマーケト ボスウオルスでElizabeth de Payneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1247年にイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアで47歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas de Payne III, Knights Templar(이)가 1200년 영국 레이스스터실르 마크트 보스워스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Thomas Payne II, Knights Templar(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Elizabeth Marguerite(은)는 25세였다. 그는 대략 1240년 영국 레이스스터실르 마크트 보스워스에서 Elizabeth de Payne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1247년 영국 레이스스터실르에서 47세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ele casou-se com Elizabeth de Payne aproximadamente 1240, em Market Bosworth, Leicestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Thomas de Payne III, Knights Templar родился в 1200 г. в местности маркет босворт, лисестиршир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Thomas Payne II, Knights Templar было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Elizabeth Marguerite, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth de Payne около 1240 г. в местности маркет босворт, лисестиршир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1247 г. в местности лисестиршир, Англия в возрасте 47 лет.
